title: Fresh Quenched Irish Buckwheat Cake
description: Light and airy, this Irish buckwheat cake is made without eggs using a fermented batter. Perfect for breakfast or a snack.

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: all-purpose flour helps to strengthen the buckwheat flour

ingredients.name: yeast
ingredients.notes: active dry yeast, instant yeast works too

ingredients.name: unflavored gelatin
ingredients.notes: helps with texture, structural integrity, and moisture retention

ingredients.name: unsweetened almond milk
ingredients.notes: room temperature milk for proper gelatin activation, not hot milk

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: cooled down, used in combination with almond milk for the right consistency

ingredients.name: cinnamon
ingredients.notes: optional, for a hint of warm flavor

instructions: Combine the buckwheat flour, all-purpose flour, yeast, sugar, and gelatin in a large mixing bowl.
In a separate container, mix the almond milk and water until well combined.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, and mix until everything is well combined.
Cover the bowl with a clean, dry towel, and let the mixture rest in a warm place for 6-8 hours, allowing the fermented batter to develop, grow the fermentation flavors
Preheat a non-stick or well-seasoned cast-iron skillet or cake pan to 375°F (190°C).
Add a thin layer of the fermented batter to the preheated pan, about 1-2 inches thick.
Flip the batter over when the edges start to set, and cook on the other side until golden brown, about 3-5 minutes on each side
The cake will be flaky, light, and airy, so be gentle when flipping it over
